component.name: Container
component.description: sbomify's production container image. VEX justifications for High advisories embedded in the osv-scanner and cosign CLI binaries that are bundled to run the OSV and signature-verification plugins. These are operator tooling invoked as subprocesses on trusted inputs, not sbomify runtime code.

vulnerabilities.description: Moby/Docker daemon authorization-plugin (AuthZ) bypass via an oversized request body: the daemon's server-side AuthZ middleware (github.com/docker/docker/pkg/authorization) skips body inspection on oversized requests. Embedded as an indirect Go-module dependency of the osv-scanner binary. Fixed in Docker Engine 29.3.1.

analysis.detail: The vulnerable code is github.com/docker/docker/pkg/authorization, the Docker daemon's server-side HTTP-API authorization middleware. docker/docker is an indirect dependency of osv-scanner (via osv-scalibr / go-containerregistry) and is only referenced by osv-scanner's container-image scanning paths, which act as a client. osv-scanner never runs a Docker daemon or its AuthZ middleware, so the vulnerable path is structurally unreachable; sbomify uses osv-scanner only for static SBOM/dependency analysis.

vulnerabilities.description: Docker/Moby daemon archive-copy handler (PUT /containers/{id}/archive, `docker cp`) resolves a decompression binary (xz/unpigz) from the running container's filesystem instead of the host, so a trojanised in-container decompressor can execute with daemon (host root) privileges. Embedded as an indirect Go-module dependency of the osv-scanner binary. Fixed in Docker Engine 29.5.1 / moby v2.0.0-beta.14.

analysis.detail: This is a Docker daemon runtime code path (the archive-upload handler spawning an external in-container decompressor), explicitly not an image-layer/manifest/tar parsing bug. osv-scanner performs static dependency/SBOM analysis: it never starts a Moby daemon, creates a container, or invokes the archive-copy/decompression path, so the vulnerable function is unreachable in sbomify.

vulnerabilities.description: Race condition (TOCTOU) in the Docker daemon's `docker cp` mount setup (github.com/docker/docker/daemon): a process inside a container can replace the bind-mount destination with a symlink between mountpoint creation and the mount() syscall, redirecting the mount to an arbitrary host path. Embedded as an indirect Go-module dependency of the osv-scanner binary. Fixed in Docker Engine 29.5.1 / moby v2.0.0-beta.14.

analysis.detail: The flaw is daemon-only TOCTOU mount-setup code, reached exclusively via `docker cp` / PUT|HEAD /containers/{id}/archive against a live daemon with a running container. osv-scanner parses lockfiles/SBOMs and image-layer metadata and never executes the daemon's copy/mount logic, so the path is structurally unreachable in sbomify.

vulnerabilities.description: Go standard library `mime` package: quadratic-complexity CPU exhaustion (DoS) in mime.WordDecoder.DecodeHeader when decoding an RFC 2047 MIME header containing many invalid encoded-words. Present because the cosign v3.1.1 binary is built with Go 1.26.3 (fixed in Go 1.25.11 / 1.26.4).

analysis.detail: mime.WordDecoder.DecodeHeader is the RFC 2047 email encoded-word decoder; it is not invoked by net/http request/response parsing and is not on cosign's execution path. cosign's communication with the OCI registry, Rekor, and Fulcio is HTTP+JSON and never decodes RFC 2047 email headers, so the vulnerable function -- though compiled into the binary's standard library -- is unreachable. cosign v3.1.1 is the latest release; sbomify will rebuild on the next cosign release compiled with Go 1.26.4+.
